steps.title: Find the shared point
steps.content: Text 1 says Benton and Sato used barriers to keep plant roots separate. Text 2 accepts that the barriers separated the roots and excluded root-to-root transmission, even while criticizing the study for allowing liquids through.

steps.title: Reject claims beyond agreement
steps.content: B requires a barrier that also blocks fungal strands, which would prevent testing a CMN and is not endorsed by both texts. C says excluding root transfer is sufficient, which Text 2 denies. D makes an unsupported claim about Nair and colleagues' results. The shared point is that Benton and Sato's barriers excluded root-to-root transfer. The answer is A.
